metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify the title-prefix resolution rule for `change:<id>` keys (e.g., strip the `change:` type tag and match against `code-review: {id}`) so the reader's resolution is unambiguous for both key types.
findings.finding: The typed key format `change:<id>` used in `latest_code_review_result` does not textually align with the Gerrit artifact title format `code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}`: resolving by 'title prefix' requires stripping the `change:` prefix to obtain `{change_id}`, but this mapping rule is never stated, leaving the reader's resolution mechanism for Gerrit artifacts under-specified and potentially contradicting 'Gerrit unchanged' in the artifact-addressing sub-bullet.
findings.location: What section — 'Artifact addressing' bullet vs. 'reader latest_code_review_result(key)' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the reader description: 'For `session:<id>`, match title prefix `code-review: session:{id}`; for `change:<id>`, match title prefix `code-review: {id} @` (stripping the `change:` tag to obtain the bare change_id).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `build_payload` will add a per-finding `decision` field (mirroring plan_review's `_slim`) or whether the reader's SURFACED-only filter uses bucket membership (`blocking`/`advisory` keys) as the proxy — the current payload has no `decision` field to filter on.
findings.finding: The SURFACED-only filtering rule for `latest_code_review_result` references `decision in {block, advisory}` as a per-finding decision vocabulary, but `code_review_result_v1`'s `build_payload` stores findings in separate top-level buckets (`blocking`, `advisory`, `coaching`) with NO per-finding `decision` field — unlike `plan_review_result_v1` which does stamp per-finding `decision`. The plan's filter protocol is structurally unresolvable against the payload it targets.
findings.location: ## What — `latest_code_review_result(key)` paragraph; AC line 'returns SURFACED-only prior findings + the deps map'; src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py `build_payload` lines 77-91
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) extend `build_payload`'s findings representation with a `decision` field (e.g. stamp `'block'` for entries from `blocking`, `'advisory'` for entries from `advisory`) so the reader's `decision in {block, advisory}` filter is directly applicable; or (b) restate the reader spec as 'return findings from the `blocking` and `advisory` buckets only' and define the return shape accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that reaching both emit paths requires either (a) threading deps through the verdict dict AND updating voter.py's separate sidecar.emit call to pass deps, or (b) moving Gerrit's emit into produce_code_review_verdict; update the plan's 'computed once in the gate' claim to name this wiring explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'computed once in the gate (produce_code_review_verdict) so BOTH local and the Gerrit voter path get it via the existing emit', but the Gerrit voter path (voter.py:222-229) calls `sidecar.emit()` DIRECTLY — it does NOT go through `produce_code_review_verdict`. The deps map computed inside produce_code_review_verdict would therefore not reach the Gerrit voter's emit call without additional wiring.
findings.location: What section: 'Computed once in the gate (produce_code_review_verdict) so BOTH local and the Gerrit voter path get it via the existing emit'; AC5: 'The deps map is computed once in the gate and reaches both local and Gerrit emits'
findings.suggested_fix: The simplest fix: store deps in the returned verdict dict (e.g. `verdict['_deps']`), then update both emit call-sites (gate_dispatch.py and voter.py) to extract and forward `deps`. Update the plan to name voter.py as a required change site.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `session_id` origin and threading protocol: specify whether it is caller-supplied via `CodeReviewRequest`, auto-generated inside `produce_code_review_verdict`, or sourced from an existing session context; and specify how it flows to `sidecar.emit` and `build_payload`.
findings.finding: `session_id` is specified as a top-level payload field and as the basis for local artifact titles, and the plan says it is computed once in `produce_code_review_verdict` so 'BOTH local and the Gerrit voter path get it via the existing emit' — but `CodeReviewRequest` (the sole input struct) has no `session_id` field, and no protocol is given for where `session_id` originates (caller-supplied? auto-generated inside the gate?), how it threads through `_assemble_code_review_run`/`_run_code_review_gate`, and how it reaches `sidecar.emit`.
findings.location: ## What — first bullet; AC line 1; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py `CodeReviewRequest` lines 455-469 and `_run_code_review_gate` lines 542-600
findings.suggested_fix: Add `session_id: str | None = None` to `CodeReviewRequest`; generate a UUID in `produce_code_review_verdict` if not supplied by the caller (local path auto-generates; Gerrit path passes None); thread it through to `sidecar.emit` and into `build_payload`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define in the plan (or inline in the AC) which `code_review_result_v1` schema field/value marks a finding as 'SURFACED' versus 'dropped', so the reader's filtering logic and its unit tests have a concrete, self-contained specification.
findings.finding: The plan mandates 'SURFACED-only' filtering in the reader and unit tests but never defines which artifact schema field (or value) distinguishes a 'surfaced' finding from a 'dropped' one. Without this, the reader's core filtering behavior is unspecified and cannot be faithfully implemented or tested.
findings.location: What section / AC #3 and #4 / 'SURFACED-only is mandatory (see old-frilly-plankton)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: "A finding is 'SURFACED' when its `decision` field is one of `{block, advisory}`; findings with any other value (e.g. `dropped`, absent) are excluded by the reader." Then update AC #3 and #4 to reference this field explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py::emit_code_review_artifact` to the Scope section as a consumer that must be updated to pass `deps` (and optionally `session_id`) to `sidecar.emit()`, OR explain the mechanism by which deps computed in `produce_code_review_verdict` is threaded through the verdict dict to the voter's emit call.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py::emit_code_review_artifact` is an external caller of `sidecar.emit()` (and transitively `build_payload`) that lives OUTSIDE the plan's stated scope dir (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`) and is not enumerated as a consumer requiring update. The plan says deps reach 'both local and Gerrit emits via the existing emit', but the Gerrit emit goes through `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` which calls `sidecar.emit()` with a fixed signature that has no `deps` or `session_id` parameter. For deps to reach the Gerrit artifact, `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` must also be updated.
findings.location: Scope section; src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py:169 (emit_code_review_artifact); src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py:94 (emit)
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) return `deps` as a field in the verdict dict from `produce_code_review_verdict` so `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` can pass it through, and add `voter.py` to Scope; or (b) re-compute deps inside `sidecar.build_payload` when `changed_files` is supplied, removing the 'once in the gate' constraint. Whichever approach is chosen, `voter.py` must be listed as an edit target.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command or unit-test case for AC5 that asserts deps is populated identically in both the local and Gerrit emit paths (e.g., a test that calls `produce_code_review_verdict` with a mock that captures both emit calls and asserts `payload['deps']` is non-empty and identical in each).
findings.finding: AC5 ('The deps map is computed once in the gate and reaches both local and Gerrit emits') has no corresponding proving command, test, or test fixture described anywhere in the plan, and is framed as a structural/implementation constraint rather than an observable outcome — an agent can satisfy all named unit tests (AC4) while computing deps twice or not delivering it to one emit path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'a test that mocks both local and Gerrit emit sinks, calls produce_code_review_verdict, and asserts payload["deps"] is a non-empty dict and identical across both captured emit calls (verifying single-computation + dual delivery).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add AC unit-test cases covering: (a) `absent` sentinel emitted for a deleted/missing changed file in the deps map; (b) deps map sha256 values match `attest._hash_file` output for present files; (c) both the local and Gerrit emit code paths receive the computed deps map (e.g., assert the payload passed to each emit contains the `deps` key).
findings.finding: The `deps` map — which carries new boundary semantics (the `absent` sentinel for created/deleted files, sha256 via `attest._hash_file`) — has no explicitly specified test coverage in the AC. The listed unit tests address surfaced-only filtering, typed-key isolation, and malformed/absent → None, but none verify (a) that the `absent` sentinel is emitted when a changed file is missing/deleted, (b) that sha256 values are correctly computed and included in the reader's returned deps, or (c) that the deps map actually reaches BOTH the local and Gerrit emit paths (the dual-emit claim in AC bullet 5 has no corresponding test criterion).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — unit tests bullet and AC bullet 5 ('deps map is computed once in the gate and reaches both local and Gerrit emits')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the unit-test AC bullet: 'deps map: absent sentinel for deleted files, correct sha256 for present files, and an integration-style assertion that the payload reaching the Gerrit voter path contains `deps` (not just the local path).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a concrete default for 'cited-file paths': e.g., 'files explicitly named in surfaced findings' (parsed from the finding's `file` field) vs. 'only changed_files' — the current phrasing leaves scope undefined.
findings.finding: The scope of `deps` includes 'cited-file paths' in addition to `changed_files`, but 'cited-file' is never defined — an implementing agent cannot determine which files qualify without guessing (files mentioned in diff hunks? files named in LLM findings text? imports reachable from changed files?).
findings.location: What section — `deps` definition; also AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(and cited-file paths)' with a stated default, e.g.: 'Scope = the review's `changed_files` union the set of file paths explicitly referenced in surfaced findings (the `file` field of each finding record); if no such field exists, scope = `changed_files` only.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the deps scope between 'What' and AC1: either add 'and cited-file paths' to AC1's deps map description, or remove it from the body if it was unintentional.
findings.finding: The 'What' section specifies the deps map scope as 'changed_files (and cited-file paths)', but AC1 describes only 'a {path: sha256} deps map built by a code-review dep collector over changed_files' — cited-file paths are omitted from the acceptance criterion, leaving their inclusion untestable and creating a contradiction between the body and the verifying criterion.
findings.location: What section (deps scope bullet) vs. Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to: '…a {path: sha256} deps map covering the review's changed_files AND any cited-file paths, built by a code-review dep collector reusing `attest._hash_file`…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify the data-flow by which `deps` travels to the Gerrit voter's `sidecar.emit` call — e.g., attach `deps` to the verdict dict inside `produce_code_review_verdict` so `build_payload` reads `verdict.get('deps')`, eliminating any need to change `voter.py`.
findings.finding: The plan says deps 'reach both local and Gerrit emits via the existing emit', but the Gerrit voter (`voter.py::emit_code_review_artifact`) calls `sidecar.emit(verdict, ...)` independently — it does not re-enter `produce_code_review_verdict` to recompute deps. The plan does not specify the data-flow mechanism by which `deps` travels from where it is computed to the Gerrit `sidecar.emit` call: whether deps are attached to the verdict dict (so `build_payload` reads `verdict.get('deps')`), added as a new `emit()` parameter, or computed separately inside the voter.
findings.location: What section – 'Computed once in the gate'; AC: 'The deps map is computed once in the gate and reaches both local and Gerrit emits'; src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py `emit_code_review_artifact`
findings.suggested_fix: Attach the computed `deps` map directly onto the verdict dict (e.g. `verdict['deps'] = deps_map`) inside `produce_code_review_verdict` before returning; `build_payload` then reads `verdict.get('deps', {})` — requiring zero changes to `voter.py` or any other caller.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the `change:<id>` typed key resolves to the most-recent artifact across ALL revisions of a change (cross-patchset novelty) or only a specific revision, and confirm this is the intended semantic for the novelty convergence consumer.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the `change:<id>` typed key for the reader but the Gerrit artifacts use title `code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}` — multiple revisions of the same change each create a distinct artifact with the same `change_id` prefix. A title-prefix match on `change:<change_id>` would return the most-recent artifact across ALL revisions, meaning a re-review of revision N could read findings from revision N-1 (a different code state). The plan says 'most-recent artifact' but does not state that `change:<id>` resolves to the correct revision's artifact for the novelty gate's use, nor specify whether the key should be `change:<change_id>` or `change:<change_id>@<revision>`.
findings.location: What section – 'Artifact addressing'; AC: 'the reader resolves by title prefix for the typed key'; src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py line 197

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the code-review dep collector's cited-path extraction is scoped to SURFACED buckets only (`blocking`/`advisory`) or all buckets (matching `attest._cited_paths`), and document this decision in the plan.
findings.finding: The `deps` map scope is 'changed_files (and cited-file paths)' but the plan does not specify which cited-path extraction protocol to use. The existing `attest._cited_paths` reads from all finding buckets including `dropped` and `overflow` — inconsistent with the SURFACED-only spirit stated elsewhere in the plan. No code-review-specific cited-path extraction protocol is defined, leaving ambiguity about whether dropped-finding citations enter the deps map.
findings.location: ## What — deps sub-bullet 'Scope = the review's changed_files (and cited-file paths)'; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py `_cited_paths` lines 101-114
findings.suggested_fix: Scope cited-path extraction to `blocking` and `advisory` buckets only for consistency with the SURFACED-only principle, and specify this explicitly in the plan's deps sub-bullet.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify explicitly whether `deps` hashing occurs inside or outside the `gate_read_root` snapshot context in `produce_code_review_verdict`, and confirm this matches `attest._hash_file`'s `base` parameter semantics for the intended reviewed-code state.
findings.finding: The plan says deps are 'Computed once in the gate (produce_code_review_verdict)' but `_run_code_review_gate` runs the workflow inside a `gate_source.gate_read_root(handle)` snapshot context. `attest._hash_file` reads raw working-tree bytes — if deps are computed INSIDE the snapshot context they hash snapshot bytes; if OUTSIDE they hash the current working tree. The plan never pins which side of the context-manager boundary the computation falls on, but this is load-bearing: the deps map is supposed to record the code STATE that was reviewed, which for the gate is the attested snapshot.
findings.location: What section – 'Computed once in the gate (produce_code_review_verdict)'; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py `_run_code_review_gate` (gate_source.gate_read_root context)
findings.suggested_fix: Compute deps inside the `gate_source.gate_read_root(handle)` context (after the verdict is available) so hashes reflect the attested snapshot bytes that were actually reviewed; pass the handle's resolved root as the `base` argument to `_hash_file`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly define whether pre-existing `code_review_result_v1` artifacts lacking `deps` are treated as usable (returning empty deps) or unusable (triggering walk-back), and add a brief rollback note (e.g., 'old artifacts without `deps` return `{}` gracefully; no reader breakage on writer rollback').
findings.finding: The plan extends the `code_review_result_v1` payload schema (adding `session_id` and `deps`) but does not specify a rollback path or expand-contract sequence: if the extended writer is deployed and then rolled back to the old writer, new readers expecting `deps` will encounter old-format artifacts, yet the reader's walk-back behavior on artifacts lacking `deps` is not defined (treat as usable with null deps, or walk back past as unusable).
findings.location: What section (payload extension) and reader description
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the reader spec: 'Artifacts validated as `code_review_result_v1` but lacking a `deps` key are treated as usable; the reader returns `{}` for `deps` in that case, ensuring safe reader deployment before or after writer rollback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan to state that the reader iterates `list_tickets(ticket_type='code_review')` and matches by title equality (not a native prefix query), mirroring voter.py's existing approach; clarify whether 'title prefix' means the artifact title starts with `code-review: session:` (type discrimination) vs exact-match on the full `code-review: session:{id}` title.
findings.finding: The plan states the reader 'resolves the artifact by title prefix' for the typed key, but `list_tickets` and `apply_ticket_filters` have no title-filter parameter. The only existing mechanism (used by voter.py:202-205) is a full linear scan of all code_review artifacts followed by string-equality matching. The plan's phrasing 'resolves by title prefix' implies a native query capability that does not exist.
findings.location: What section: 'local artifacts use title `code-review: session:{session_id}`… the reader resolves by title prefix for the typed key'; AC: 'the reader resolves by title prefix for the typed key'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'resolves by title prefix' with 'iterates list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review") and matches title by equality on the full typed-key title string, mirroring voter.py:202-205'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `latest_code_review_result` uses exact-title match (consistent with `voter.py`) or true prefix match, justify the divergence if prefix is chosen, and verify that `rebar.list_tickets` supports this lookup mode efficiently.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel title-prefix resolution pattern for `latest_code_review_result(key)` — resolving artifacts by title prefix (`code-review: session:{id}` or `code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}`) — but the existing Gerrit voter path uses exact-title matching (`str(t.get('title')) == title` in `voter.py:204`). The plan does not acknowledge this divergence, verify that `rebar.list_tickets` can support prefix-filtered or full-scan lookup efficiently, or justify why prefix-based resolution is necessary over exact-title matching.
findings.location: What section — 'reader resolves by title prefix for the typed key'; AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Use exact-title match (consistent with the voter path) plus typed-key disambiguation by title equality, rather than prefix matching — this avoids ambiguity between session IDs that share a common prefix and aligns with the established codebase pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expose `_hash_file` (or an equivalent `hash_file(path) -> str` helper) as a stable, non-private API surface (e.g. a public function in `attest` or a shared `hashing` utility module) so the cross-module coupling has a defined contract.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly couples `sidecar.py` to `attest._hash_file` — a private (underscore-prefixed) function in another module — with no interface boundary, re-export, or adapter. This is a fragile cross-component dependency: any internal refactor of `attest` silently breaks the code-review path.
findings.location: What section / AC 1: '…reusing only the `attest._hash_file` primitive'
findings.suggested_fix: Promote `attest._hash_file` to `attest.hash_file` (or extract to a shared `rebar.utils.hashing` module) and update the plan's AC to reference the public name, making the dependency explicit and stable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the error-handling posture for `_hash_file` failures in the deps computation: define whether the gate should fail-open (emit artifact with partial/empty deps and log a warning), fail-closed (raise and skip emit), or use the `absent` sentinel for unreadable files — and document the chosen behavior in the AC.
findings.finding: The deps computation (calling `attest._hash_file` per changed file) inside `produce_code_review_verdict` has no specified error-handling posture: if any file read fails (file deleted between diff and hash, permission error, etc.), the plan does not say whether the gate should fail-closed, emit the artifact without deps, or log-and-continue with partial deps.
findings.location: ## What — deps computation seam in produce_code_review_verdict; AC: 'The deps map is computed once in the gate and reaches both local and Gerrit emits'
findings.suggested_fix: Adopt fail-open: catch any exception from `_hash_file` per file, substitute the `absent` sentinel, and emit a log.warning with the path and error. This preserves the emit for both local and Gerrit paths while making the gap visible.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: specify that reader walk-back exhaustion and schema-mismatch events are logged at WARNING level, and that hash-computation failures in the gate are logged at WARNING with the offending file path, so operators can detect systematic failures.
findings.finding: No observability (logging, metrics, or tracing) is specified for the new failure points introduced: hash computation failures during deps collection, and reader resolution failures (title-prefix miss, schema mismatch, walk-back exhaustion).
findings.location: ## What — reader `latest_code_review_result`; deps computation seam in produce_code_review_verdict

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE/cleanup policy for `session:{session_id}` artifacts (e.g., TTL, max-per-session cap, or explicit deletion on session close) or explicitly document that cleanup is deferred to paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish and update that task's scope accordingly.
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is defined for `session:{session_id}` local artifacts, which will accumulate indefinitely in the artifact store as sessions are created.
findings.location: ## What — 'Local artifacts use title `code-review: session:{session_id}`'; ## Scope — 'Out of scope: local persistence wiring (paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `latest_code_review_result(key)` has at least one in-scope consumer wired in this plan; if the only consumer (blameless-grindable-noctule) remains out-of-scope, consider deferring the reader to that plan, or explicitly justify the 'build it now, wire it later' sequencing decision.
findings.finding: The `latest_code_review_result(key)` reader — including its typed-key dispatch scheme (`session:<id>` vs `change:<id>`) — has no confirmed in-scope consumer at plan delivery time. Its only named consumer, the `region_gated_floor` (blameless-grindable-noctule), is explicitly placed out-of-scope in the Scope section. This violates YAGNI (serves a hypothetical future consumer) and fails Rule-of-Three (zero call-sites at delivery).
findings.location: Scope section ('Out of scope: … the region_gated_floor consumer (blameless-grindable-noctule)') and What section ('Add a reader `latest_code_review_result(key)`')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `surfaced_findings()` (or the equivalent surfaced-only filter from old-frilly-plankton) is merged and present in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` before implementing `latest_code_review_result`; if not, either sequence the dependency explicitly or define the filter inline with a reference to the pending merge.
findings.finding: The plan treats SURFACED-only filtering (per old-frilly-plankton) as settled behavior the reader can rely on, but `surfaced_findings()` — the helper that was to implement that filtering — does not exist in the codebase yet (grep returns zero matches in src/). The bug ticket is `in_progress` with a comment describing an implementation, but no code is present, meaning the prerequisite contract the plan builds on is unverified.
findings.location: What section: 'SURFACED-ONLY is mandatory (see old-frilly-plankton) so dropped findings never re-enter the novelty prior set'; AC: 'latest_code_review_result(key) returns SURFACED-only prior findings'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a hard prerequisite note: 'Depends on old-frilly-plankton being merged (surfaced_findings helper must be in sidecar.py).' Alternatively, define the filter (decision in {block, advisory}) inline in latest_code_review_result without importing the helper, and note it should be refactored to use the shared helper once merged.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in the plan that `latest_code_review_result(session:<id>)` returns None unconditionally until `paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish` ships (local artifact creation), and note this dependency so the reader consumer (blameless-grindable-noctule) is not wired against a branch that always returns None.
findings.finding: The plan defers local artifact creation (titled `code-review: session:{session_id}`) to out-of-scope story `paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish`, but the `latest_code_review_result(key)` reader (a deliverable of THIS plan) resolves by title prefix for `session:` keys. With no local artifact creation in scope, the reader will always return None for `session:` keys until that story ships — yet the plan's AC2 and AC3 specify and test this reader without acknowledging that it is dead code for `session:` keys in isolation.
findings.location: Scope section ('Out of scope: local persistence wiring (paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish)'); AC2; AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` (specifically `_run_code_review_gate`'s `sidecar.emit()` call site at line ~595) to the Scope section as a required edit target for threading `deps` and `session_id` into the sidecar emit.
findings.finding: `gate_dispatch._run_code_review_gate` (in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py`) is an external caller of `sidecar.emit()` that must be updated to pass `deps` and optionally `session_id`, but it is not listed in the Scope section. The plan says deps are 'computed once in the gate' in `produce_code_review_verdict` — `_run_code_review_gate` is where that emit currently lives — so this file IS an implicit edit target.
findings.location: Scope section; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:592-595
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py (_run_code_review_gate seam)' to the Scope bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify why `attest._hash_file` is accessed directly as a private symbol (or re-export it as `attest.hash_file` / move it to a shared utility) before committing to cross-module private coupling.
findings.finding: The plan couples `code_review/sidecar.py` directly to `plan_review/attest._hash_file` — a Python-private (`_`-prefixed), non-exported implementation detail — with no interface boundary, re-export, or adapter, and without considering the alternative of co-locating the primitive or re-exporting it. This is a novel cross-package private-API coupling with no acknowledged prior art or justification for why direct access is preferred over a thin shared utility.
findings.location: What section — '`deps` computation reuses only the `attest._hash_file` primitive'
findings.suggested_fix: Re-export the primitive as a public function (e.g. `plan_review.attest.hash_file`) or move it to a shared `rebar.llm.hashing` utility module, so both callers import a stable public surface.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assert on the post-rollback schema state: either (a) bump the schema version to `code_review_result_v2` so old readers can skip/ignore unknown-version artifacts, or (b) explicitly document that all existing `code_review_result_v1` consumers use lenient deserialization (unknown keys ignored) and will degrade gracefully to the pre-new-fields behavior when `session_id`/`deps` are absent.
findings.finding: The plan extends the `code_review_result_v1` payload with new fields (`session_id`, `deps`) without bumping the schema version, and does not assert on the artifact-store state after a rollback — specifically, what happens when old-code readers encounter new-format artifacts that were written during the deployment window.
findings.location: ## What — `build_payload` extension; ## Acceptance Criteria AC1; src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Rollback safety' note in the plan: 'All existing readers of `code_review_result_v1` use dict-based lenient deserialization; unknown top-level keys are ignored. After rollback, new-format artifacts remain in the store but old readers treat them as schema-v1 records with extra ignored keys — no consumer breakage. New-reader code is also removed, so `latest_code_review_result` is no longer callable.' If that claim cannot be verified, bump to `code_review_result_v2` so old readers can short-circuit on version mismatch.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test-file location (e.g., `tests/llm/code_review/test_sidecar.py`) to the Scope section to cover AC4's unit-test scenarios, ensuring the described work has a corresponding deliverable.
findings.finding: AC4 specifies concrete unit-test scenarios (surfaced-only filtering, typed-key isolation, malformed/absent → None) but neither the 'What' section nor the 'Scope' section describes where or how these tests are written; no test file or test module is named in the scope.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4 vs. Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'tests/llm/code_review/test_sidecar.py — unit tests for surfaced-only filtering, session:/change: key isolation, and malformed/absent artifact handling.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation update (ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design-doc) capturing the typed-key artifact-addressing scheme and the surfaced-only reader invariant as a first-class architectural decision.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new typed-key artifact-addressing contract (`session:<id>` vs `change:<id>`, title-prefix resolution, surfaced-only reader posture) — a novel architectural decision — but mentions no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry to capture it.
findings.location: What section / AC 2 & 3: typed-key addressing and `latest_code_review_result` reader contract

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the title-prefix addressing approach (e.g., 'mirrors the existing voter.py idempotency title scan, keeping the lookup stateless and avoiding compiled-state coupling').
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for the title-prefix artifact-addressing mechanism (resolving by scanning artifact titles via `list_tickets`) over alternatives such as querying by a dedicated payload field (`session_id` or `change_id` top-level) or using the compiled state's ticket type filter. The absence of a positive rationale for this design choice leaves the implementer without guidance on why title-prefix was chosen over a field-query approach.
findings.location: What section – 'Artifact addressing' and 'Add a reader latest_code_review_result(key)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'Title-prefix resolution mirrors the voter.py idempotency check (`list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review")` + title match) — stateless, no compiled-state dependency, consistent with the existing codebase pattern.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Validate (via a spike or explicit rationale) that `cites_prior_location` is semantically transferable to source-file/line region matching before committing to reusing the unmodified NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS contract for code-review novelty scoring.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the existing `cites_prior_location` NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS sub-question — which is hardcoded in `decide.py` as 'same plan location/section as that prior finding' and in `plan_review_novelty.md` as 'same plan location / section / AC line' — can be repurposed for source-file/line region semantics in the new code-review novelty prompt. This is an unverified capability assertion: the LLM is being asked to use a contract field whose name and existing prompt wording are plan-specific for a structurally different domain (file:line citations vs. ticket section references), with no spike or validation.
findings.location: What section — 'adapts cites_prior_location to source-file/line region semantics'; dependent story blameless-grindable-noctule AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the 'title prefix' artifact addressing model with a concrete protocol grounded in the actual `append_event`/ticket-dir storage model — specify how `session_id` vs `change_id` keys are encoded (e.g. as payload fields) and how the reader distinguishes session-keyed from change-keyed artifacts when walking the ticket directory.
findings.finding: The artifact addressing section introduces `title` and 'title prefix' resolution (`code-review: session:{session_id}`, `code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}`) as if the storage layer has a titled-artifact registry. The actual storage primitive is `append_event(target_ticket, EVENT_TYPE, payload, tracker)` — events are stored as timestamp-prefixed JSON files in a ticket directory, indexed by ticket_id and event type, with NO title field. 'Title prefix' resolution is a pink-elephant concept with no corresponding construct in the codebase.
findings.location: ## What — 'Artifact addressing' paragraph; src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py `emit` lines 94-126; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py `latest_review_result` lines 113-166

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
